screenshot_status: SUCCESS — desktop 1280x800 and mobile 375x812 captured via Puppeteer (single-process mode). Screenshots saved to /workspace/screenshot-desktop.png and /workspace/screenshot-mobile.png.

visual_observations.desktop: Clean, polished dark theme. Bloberto in center top manager desk with CVO badge. Maya/Dave/Sofia top row, Maya/Carlos/Dave bottom row. Coffee corner, midnight clock, standup label, and vibe shockwave animation all rendering. Shortcuts hint visible bottom-right. No layout breaks detected at 1280px.
visual_observations.mobile: Worker names (MAYA, DAVE, SOFIA) are NOW VISIBLE — the mobile fix worked. Header is single-column and readable. Office floor scales down reasonably. Tab navigation remains accessible. Stats bar not visible in 812px viewport (likely below tab area, only visible on scroll). Puppeteer shows shortcuts toast because it uses a mouse pointer — real touch devices correctly hide it via pointer: coarse media query.
visual_observations.regressions_vs_31: None detected. Six high-priority bugs from #31 were fixed. Office.jsx grew by 30 lines (1,483 → 1,513). Duplicate @media (max-width: 480px) blocks increased from 2 to 3.

fix_verification.inter_font: FIXED ✅ — index.html line 8 now has <link href='https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@400;500;600;700&display=swap' rel='stylesheet'>. After 4+ reviews this is finally done.
fix_verification.mobile_worker_names: FIXED ✅ — All three @media (max-width: 480px) blocks in Office.css now set .char__name { display: block }. Dead font-size-on-hidden-element bug also cleaned up. Names visible in mobile screenshot.
fix_verification.stats_bar_overflow: FIXED ✅ — App.css line 118 now uses grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(140px, 1fr)). Handles any number of conditional cards gracefully.
fix_verification.stuck_card_keyboard: PARTIALLY FIXED ⚠️ — App.jsx now has tabIndex={0}, role='button', and onKeyDown for Enter/Space (lines 459-467). However, the element still has NO aria-label. Inner text reads '⚠️ Stuck' and worker info — screen readers will announce the emoji literally. Good enough for keyboard nav; aria-label still missing for clarity.
fix_verification.task_tag_focus_visible: FIXED ✅ — App.css line 920 now has .task-tag:focus-visible rule. Keyboard focus is now visible on filter chips.
fix_verification.ping_aria_live: FIXED ✅ — Office.jsx line 1162 now has {pingReaction ?? ''}. Screen readers will announce ping reactions. Dave gets credit.
fix_verification.settimeout_leak_ping: NOT FIXED ❌ — Office.jsx line 1093: setTimeout(() => setPingReaction(null), 3000) is still raw, not stored in a ref, and has no cleanup. The pingTimerRef at line 1081 only cleans up pingedId, NOT pingReaction. Unmount during the 3s window = state update on dead component.

score_notes: Responsiveness and accessibility both bumped from #31 thanks to the six-fix sweep. Code quality stays at 2 — Office.jsx is now 1,513 lines (grew again), three fragmented 480px media query blocks, duplicate CSS still present. Performance docked for constants inside render, multiple uncleaned setTimeouts, and a 1500-line single-file component that will never be fast to parse.

suggestions.title: [5+ REVIEWS] setPingReaction setTimeout not stored in ref — will fire on unmounted tree
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1093: `setTimeout(() => setPingReaction(null), 3000)` is a raw timer with no ref, no cleanup, and no cancellation. The existing pingTimerRef only tracks the 700ms pingedId timer. If Office unmounts while this 3-second timer is live, React will attempt to call setPingReaction on a dead component. Fix: add a `pingReactionTimerRef = useRef(null)` (or reuse pingTimerRef and extend its cleanup), store the timer ID, clear it at the top of handleCharClick, and clear it in a useEffect cleanup returning `() => clearTimeout(pingReactionTimerRef.current)`.

suggestions.title: [2 REVIEWS] VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS uses key 'crushing-it' but vibe.js returns 'crushing'
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 109: VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS['crushing-it'] = '☀️ Clear skies — team at full strength'. But getTeamVibeKey() in vibe.js returns 'crushing' (not 'crushing-it'). So VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS[vibe] at lines 1483-1484 always returns undefined for the most common vibe, and both the title attribute and aria-label on .vibe-weather fall back to the bare string 'crushing'. Fix is one character: change the key from 'crushing-it' to 'crushing'. This is also present in VIBE_WEATHER_ICONS at line 117 which already correctly uses 'crushing' — the inconsistency is only in VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS.

suggestions.title: Ghost (OOO) Character is focusable with no role or aria-label
suggestions.description: The Character component always renders with tabIndex={0} (Office.jsx line 644). Ghost variant characters (roster members who are OOO) are rendered without an onClick handler, so they get no role='button', no aria-label, and no tooltip (extraProps skipped for ghost variant at line 623). Result: keyboard users tab into an unlabeled interactive-looking element that does nothing meaningful. Fix: either add tabIndex={-1} when !handleClick AND variant==='ghost', or add an explicit aria-label like `{worker.name} — out of office` when variant==='ghost'. The ghost-ooo-bubble already has the right content; expose it to a11y.

suggestions.title: stat-card--stuck missing aria-label — emoji read literally by screen readers
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 452-476: the stuck card now has tabIndex={0}, role='button', and onKeyDown (good work Dave). But there's no aria-label. The inner text is '⚠️ Stuck' + worker name. Most screen readers will announce the warning emoji literally as 'warning sign' or 'exclamation'. Add aria-label={`Stuck worker: ${stuckWorkers.length === 1 ? stuckWorkers[0].name : `${stuckWorkers.length} workers stuck`}. Click to scroll to card.`} so the announcement is unambiguous.

suggestions.title: [2 REVIEWS] char-hover-card overflows viewport on rightmost desk characters
suggestions.description: Office.css line 1209: .char-hover-card uses position: absolute; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); bottom: 110%. Characters on the right side of the office (Sofia at ~left:57%) will have the hover card extend past the right edge of the office-floor container and get clipped. Fix: add a CSS custom property or use CSS @clamp / contain logic. Simplest fix: add .char--rightmost .char-hover-card { left: auto; right: 0; transform: none; } or apply data-side='right' to rightmost desk characters and add an overriding rule.

suggestions.title: [2 REVIEWS] Duplicate .office-floor[data-vibe=...] CSS rules in Office.css
suggestions.description: Office.css lines 91-95 declare .office-floor[data-vibe='crushing'] etc. WITHOUT quotes. Lines 97-114 declare the same selectors WITH quotes, adding --grid-line custom properties. The quoted set (97-114) is the complete one; the unquoted set (91-95) is dead weight that will never match modern CSS parsers. Delete lines 91-95 entirely. Net: removes 5 duplicate declarations, eliminates specificity ambiguity, and makes it clear the custom properties are always applied.

suggestions.title: [2 REVIEWS] Hoist component-level constants out of render body
suggestions.description:
Three objects are recreated on every render:
• IDLE_BUBBLE_EMOJIS (Office.jsx line 482) inside Character()
• ERROR_BUBBLE_MESSAGES (line 498) inside Character()
• FULL_IDLE_MESSAGES (line 1064) inside Office()
These are static — they never change. Move them to module scope, above the component function. Zero effort, eliminates unnecessary allocation on every render tick. With a 3-second polling interval and multiple character instances, this adds up.

suggestions.title: [5+ REVIEWS] Split Office.jsx — now at 1,513 lines and still growing
suggestions.description: Office.jsx is now 1,513 lines (grew 30 lines since review #31). CharacterAvatar, Character, AwaySign, WindowElement, ConferenceTable, VibeShockwave, WallClock, and Office are all in one file. Every PR review of this file requires scrolling through 1500 lines to find the relevant component. Suggested split: src/office/CharacterAvatar.jsx, src/office/Character.jsx, src/office/OfficeFurniture.jsx (Window, Table, Clock), src/office/Office.jsx (main orchestrator, ~300 lines). This is a dev experience debt item — not a bug, but every new bug report will be harder to fix as this grows.

suggestions.title: Three fragmented @media (max-width: 480px) blocks in Office.css with duplicate rules
suggestions.description: Office.css now has three separate @media (max-width: 480px) blocks (lines 1133, 2160, 2311). This creates duplicate rule declarations: .office-wrap appears twice, .char__name appears twice, .wb-vibe-text appears THREE times. The block at line 2311 carries a misleading comment '/* === Mobile Office Scale Fix (Luna Review #41) === */' — Luna is a character, not a review, and we're on review 32, not 41. Merge all three blocks into one at the end of Office.css, deduplicate the rules, and delete the nonsense comment.

suggestions.title: Plant toast uses aria-live but lacks role='status'
suggestions.description: The plant milestone toast (Office.jsx line 1163) uses aria-live='polite' which is correct, but has no role='status'. Adding role='status' provides stronger semantic meaning for AT that prioritize role over aria-live. Also the toast uses inline position/transform styles — it should use a .plant-toast CSS class consistent with the rest of the codebase.

suggestions.title: lone-survivor-label uses 7 inline styles — should be a CSS class
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1422: the lone-survivor-label element has style={{ fontSize: '0.45rem', opacity: 0.6, position: 'absolute', bottom: '-0.6rem', left: '50%', transform: 'translateX(-50%)', whiteSpace: 'nowrap', color: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.7)' }}. That's 7 inline style properties, which can't be overridden by media queries, themes, or reduced-motion rules. Create .lone-survivor-label in Office.css and remove the style prop.

suggestions.title: Move VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS and VIBE_WEATHER_ICONS out of Office.jsx into utils/constants.js
suggestions.description: These two lookup objects (Office.jsx lines 108-122) are pure data with no component dependencies. They belong in src/utils/constants.js alongside ROLE_EMOJIS, ROLE_COLORS, etc. This reduces Office.jsx line count and makes the vibe key mapping auditable in one place — where the VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS 'crushing-it' vs 'crushing' bug would have been easier to catch alongside getTeamVibe().

suggestions.title: Desk SVG monitor screens lack aria-label for keyboard users inspecting desks
suggestions.description: The inline SVG monitor screens in Office.jsx (DESK_SCREEN_SVG, lines 1322-1329, and the fallback SVG at line 1328) have aria-hidden='true' on the fallback but many DESK_SCREEN_SVG entries have no aria attribute at all. Workers with custom screens (Carlos, Maya, Dave etc.) have purely decorative SVGs that should be aria-hidden='true' to prevent screen reader confusion. Audit DESK_SCREEN_SVG and ensure all entries have aria-hidden='true'.
